title: F1 in Belgium: Machine learning algorithms are ruining the sport
excerpt: Many will say Spa is the best racetrack on earth, but not for these F1 cars.
content_html:
I tried to be positive

Many will say Spa is the best racetrack on earth, but not for these F1 cars.

Kimi Antonelli approaches the famous Eau Rouge corner. Credit: Nicolas Economou/NurPhoto via Getty Images

Kimi Antonelli approaches the famous Eau Rouge corner. Credit: Nicolas Economou/NurPhoto via Getty Images

Formula 1 returned to the Spa-Francorchamps circuit for the Belgium Grand Prix this weekend. This ribbon of tarmac in the Ardennes is one of the sport’s historic venues, featuring in the first F1 season way back in 1950. But as yesterday’s race showed, the current F1 machinery is utterly ill-suited to this dramatic venue.

As we saw at Suzuka in Japan earlier this year, a 2026 F1 battery is able to power the race car’s electric motor for only a few seconds a lap; for much of the rest of the lap, the energy-starved cars have less power going to their rear wheels than in the junior F2 feeder series.

The best race track in the world

For many, including much of the current F1 grid, Spa is considered perhaps the planet’s greatest race track. In fact, both Spa and the Belgian Grand Prix predate the F1 world championship, with its first race held in 1922. Back then, the circuit was a 9-mile loop of public roads, taking in the towns of Malmedy and Stavelot before returning to Francorchamps. In the postwar years, the layout shrank slightly to 8.7 miles (14.1 km) and was ferociously fast, with average lap speeds of up to 150 mph (240 km/h)—and ferociously dangerous, given cars with huge amounts of power and minimal concessions to safety.

The Spa we know and love today was reconfigured in 1979, cutting out about half of the original layout, though at 4.3 miles (6.9 km), it’s still the longest track on the F1 calendar. Interestingly, some of the circuit remained as public roads until 2000, when it became a dedicated race track only. Eau Rouge and Raidillon are its two most famous corners—a quick left, then right, that climbs 77 feet (24 m) of steep hill. But in the era of high downforce race cars, other corners like Pouhon have become greater tests of skill.

Not this year. As I’ve detailed more than a few times now, the current breed of F1 car is not at all suited to tracks with high-speed corners. Because the hybrid side of the new power units provides such little energy, going flat out all the time is not the fastest way to complete a lap. Corners that once required a driver’s commitment and maybe a little bravery to drive at the limit are now coasted into to allow the engine to divert some of its power to recharge the battery. Obviously, the drivers aren’t thrilled.

“For most of sector two, you run just on the engine,” said Red Bull’s Max Verstappen. “So what is that? 450 bhp, 500 bhp? Something like that, which is, I guess, more or less what a Formula 3 car has but with F1 downforce. So you can imagine, of course, that that is not very exciting to drive.”

“But honestly, I don’t want to sit here and complain again because probably someone will shoot me outside the door,” he joked.

Rumors continue to swirl about Verstappen’s future. As he is not in the top two of the driver’s championship, he could potentially activate an escape clause in his Red Bull contract, and some think a move to McLaren could be possible. A complete F1 sabbatical is another alternative, as Verstappen has made no secret of his desire to win some of the world’s other great races outside of F1, like the 24-hour endurance races at Le Mans, the Nürburgring, and, yes, Spa.

While I’d love to see Verstappen in more GT3 and possibly prototypes, I’m guessing he’ll stay in F1, and with Red Bull. “I’m mentally just adjusting to it. I’m trying to make the best out of it,” he told the media after qualifying. “Even though, of course, it’s not what I like, not what I love to do in Formula 1. But I can also sit at home and drive nothing. But that also doesn’t do anything. So I’m just trying my best.”

That qualifying session went rather well for the Dutch driver, too. His teammate, saddled with a grid penalty for changing an engine, helped punch a hole through the air for Verstappen, giving him a tow through the final sector of the track, letting Verstappen claim second on the grid. On pole? Mercedes’ Kimi Antonelli yet again, with a margin of three tenths of a second to the Red Bull.

F1 is another thing AI has ruined

Things did not go nearly as well for Antonelli’s teammate, George Russell. Declared this year’s champion-in-waiting before the start of the season, the British driver has been comprehensively outdriven by his teenage teammate this year. But Antonelli’s ease with the new cars and Russell’s discomfort with having to adopt a radically new driving style can’t explain the half-second gap between the pair in qualifying.

Over the weekend, the blame seemed to shift from Russell’s car to the driver, then back to the car again. There was—and is—something definitely wrong with Russell’s car, which was clearly slower than its sister car on several flat-out sections.

The self-learning AI systems that teams are now using to manage their energy deployment strategies across a lap are looking increasingly likely to blame. By their nature, they’re fairly opaque to the driver. And because the electrical energy is so limited—just 4 MJ capacity in the battery—even tiny deployments to fill in extra torque to help the V6 or from getting a bit of wheelspin is enough to throw off the energy management and leave a driver with a fraction of the power they thought they should have.

“My whole focus for the last 36 hours has been on straight-line speed. It hasn’t been focused on the set-up, tires, or anything, because we’re all trying to solve what is going on,” Russell told the media after qualifying.

“Even my last lap, for some reason, I lost another tenth and a half to myself, just on the straight,” he said. “And you’re watching on your steering wheel, [you’re] losing speed when you’re full gas on the straight. You feel powerless. We don’t know what’s going on. I don’t think it’s the power unit, to be honest. My team are really, really on it now, to try and solve it.”

His team did not solve it, and his car was visibly slower than the others as they flew down the Kemmel Straight toward the right-left of Les Combes. (Incidentally, isn’t it wonderful how all of Spa’s corners have actual names and aren’t just referred to as turn 1, turn 2, etc.?) As all the drivers lifted and coasted into what was previously a heavy braking zone, Russell regenerated enough energy to attempt an overtake on Lewis Hamilton’s Ferrari. Hamilton, minding his own business, had nowhere to go, and Russell clipped him and then spun into the gravel trap. An expletive-filled radio tirade back to the Mercedes garage followed, with Russell furious that yet again he’s rewarded with a DNF due to his car.

Lewis Hamilton would have been justified in firing off a few expletives himself. Despite the turn 1 collision being clearly a racing incident, the stewards decided it was somehow Hamilton’s fault and issued the driver a 5-second penalty.

The race was won, as so many others this season, by Antonelli. Verstappen thrilled his many fans in the crowd by briefly passing Antonelli at the start, a position he couldn’t hold. Antonelli was in the lead by Les Combes, and that’s where he finished the race, notching up his sixth win of the season. Although there were a couple of memorable overtakes, the 2026 race was far from a classic; it was one more sad example of how the new ruleset’s cars just don’t work properly on sport’s most popular race tracks. I guess I picked the wrong year to plan a trip to Monza for the Italian Grand Prix in September.

Jonathan is the Automotive Editor at Ars Technica. He has a BSc and PhD in Pharmacology. In 2014 he decided to indulge his lifelong passion for the car by leaving the National Human Genome Research Institute and launching Ars Technica's automotive coverage. He lives in Washington, DC.

115 Comments

But as yesterday’s race showed, the current F1 machinery is utterly ill-suited to this dramatic venue.

sentences.en: Formula 1 returned to the Spa-Francorchamps circuit for the Belgium Grand Prix this weekend.
sentences.zh: 一级方程式赛车本周末重返斯帕-弗朗科尔尚赛道，参加比利时大奖赛。

sentences.en: This ribbon of tarmac in the Ardennes is one of the sport’s historic venues, featuring in the first F1 season way back in 1950.
sentences.zh: 这条位于阿登地区的停机坪带是这项运动历史悠久的场地之一，早在 1950 年的第一个 F1 赛季就曾在此举办过。

sentences.en: But as yesterday’s race showed, the current F1 machinery is utterly ill-suited to this dramatic venue.
sentences.zh: 但正如昨天的比赛所表明的那样，当前的 F1 机械完全不适合这个戏剧性的场地。

blocks.html_en: As we saw at Suzuka in Japan earlier this year, a 2026 F1 battery is able to power the race car’s electric motor for only a few seconds a lap; for much of the rest of the lap, the energy-starved cars have less power going to their rear wheels than in the junior F2 feeder series.

sentences.en: As we saw at Suzuka in Japan earlier this year, a 2026 F1 battery is able to power the race car’s electric motor for only a few seconds a lap; for much of the rest of the lap, the energy-starved cars have less power going to their rear wheels than in the junior F2 feeder series.
sentences.zh: 正如我们今年早些时候在日本铃鹿车展上看到的那样，2026 F1 电池每圈只能为赛车的电动机提供几秒钟的动力；在剩下的一圈中，这些能源匮乏的赛车后轮的动力比初级 F2 支线系列赛要少。

sentences.en: Back then, the circuit was a 9-mile loop of public roads, taking in the towns of Malmedy and Stavelot before returning to Francorchamps.
sentences.zh: 当时，这条赛道是一条 9 英里长的公路环路，经过马尔梅迪和斯塔沃洛镇，然后返回弗朗科尔尚。

sentences.en: In the postwar years, the layout shrank slightly to 8.7 miles (14.1 km) and was ferociously fast, with average lap speeds of up to 150 mph (240 km/h)—and ferociously dangerous, given cars with huge amounts of power and minimal concessions to safety.
sentences.zh: 战后几年，布局略微缩小至 8.7 英里（14.1 公里），并且速度极快，平均单圈速度高达 150 英里/小时（240 公里/小时），而且由于汽车拥有巨大的动力和对安全性的最小让步，因此非常危险。

sentences.en: Interestingly, some of the circuit remained as public roads until 2000, when it became a dedicated race track only.
sentences.zh: 有趣的是，直到 2000 年，部分赛道仍然是公共道路，之后才成为专用赛道。

sentences.en: Eau Rouge and Raidillon are its two most famous corners—a quick left, then right, that climbs 77 feet (24 m) of steep hill.
sentences.zh: Eau Rouge 和 Raidillon 是其最著名的两个弯道 - 快速向左，然后向右，爬上 77 英尺（24 m）的陡峭山坡。

sentences.en: But in the era of high downforce race cars, other corners like Pouhon have become greater tests of skill.
sentences.zh: 但在高下压力赛车时代，像普洪这样的其他弯道已经成为对技术的更大考验。

Something like that, which is, I guess, more or less what a Formula 3 car has but with F1 downforce.

As he is not in the top two of the driver’s championship, he could potentially activate an escape clause in his Red Bull contract, and some think a move to McLaren could be possible. A complete F1 sabbatical is another alternative, as Verstappen has made no secret of his desire to win some of the world’s other great races outside of F1, like the 24-hour endurance races at Le Mans, the Nürburgring, and, yes, Spa.

sentences.en: Rumors continue to swirl about Verstappen’s future.
sentences.zh: 关于维斯塔潘未来的谣言仍在不断流传。

sentences.en: As he is not in the top two of the driver’s championship, he could potentially activate an escape clause in his Red Bull contract, and some think a move to McLaren could be possible.
sentences.zh: 由于他不在车手积分榜前两名，他可能会激活红牛合同中的豁免条款，一些人认为转会迈凯轮是可能的。

sentences.en: A complete F1 sabbatical is another alternative, as Verstappen has made no secret of his desire to win some of the world’s other great races outside of F1, like the 24-hour endurance races at Le Mans, the Nürburgring, and, yes, Spa.
sentences.zh: 完全的 F1 休假是另一种选择，因为维斯塔潘毫不掩饰他想要赢得 F1 之外世界上其他一些伟大比赛的渴望，比如勒芒 24 小时耐力赛、纽博格林赛道，当然还有斯帕赛道。

blocks.html_en: While I’d love to see Verstappen in more GT3 and possibly prototypes, I’m guessing he’ll stay in F1, and with Red Bull. “I’m mentally just adjusting to it. I’m trying to make the best out of it,” he told the media after qualifying. “Even though, of course, it’s not what I like, not what I love to do in Formula 1. But I can also sit at home and drive nothing. But that also doesn’t do anything. So I’m just trying my best.”

sentences.en: While I’d love to see Verstappen in more GT3 and possibly prototypes, I’m guessing he’ll stay in F1, and with Red Bull.
sentences.zh: 虽然我很想看到维斯塔潘参加更多 GT3 比赛，甚至可能参加原型车比赛，但我猜他会留在 F1 和红牛车队。

sentences.en: “I’m mentally just adjusting to it.
sentences.zh: “我只是在精神上适应它。

sentences.en: I’m trying to make the best out of it,” he told the media after qualifying.
sentences.zh: 我正在努力充分利用它，”他在排位赛后告诉媒体。

sentences.en: “Even though, of course, it’s not what I like, not what I love to do in Formula 1.
sentences.zh: “当然，尽管这不是我喜欢的，也不是我在一级方程式赛车中喜欢做的。

sentences.en: But I can also sit at home and drive nothing.
sentences.zh: 但我也可以坐在家里什么都不开车。

sentences.en: But that also doesn’t do anything.
sentences.zh: 但这也没有任何作用。

sentences.en: So I’m just trying my best.”
sentences.zh: 所以我只是尽力而为。”

blocks.html_en: Audi’s Gabriel Bortoleto is proving to be something of a whiz at Spa. He finished eighth, earning himself and Audi another four points in the process.

sentences.en: Audi’s Gabriel Bortoleto is proving to be something of a whiz at Spa.
sentences.zh: 事实证明，奥迪的加布里埃尔·博尔托莱托 (Gabriel Bortoleto) 是斯帕的天才。

sentences.en: He finished eighth, earning himself and Audi another four points in the process.
sentences.zh: 他获得第八名，并在此过程中为自己和奥迪赢得了另外四分。

sentences.en: Audi’s Gabriel Bortoleto is proving to be something of a whiz at Spa.
sentences.zh: 事实证明，奥迪的加布里埃尔·博尔托莱托 (Gabriel Bortoleto) 是斯帕的天才。

sentences.en: He finished eighth, earning himself and Audi another four points in the process.
sentences.zh: 他获得第八名，并在此过程中为自己和奥迪赢得了另外四分。

blocks.html_en: Audi’s Gabriel Bortoleto is proving to be something of a whiz at Spa. He finished eighth, earning himself and Audi another four points in the process.

sentences.en: Audi’s Gabriel Bortoleto is proving to be something of a whiz at Spa.
sentences.zh: 事实证明，奥迪的加布里埃尔·博尔托莱托 (Gabriel Bortoleto) 是斯帕的天才。

sentences.en: He finished eighth, earning himself and Audi another four points in the process.
sentences.zh: 他获得第八名，并在此过程中为自己和奥迪赢得了另外四分。

blocks.html_en: That qualifying session went rather well for the Dutch driver, too. His teammate, saddled with a grid penalty for changing an engine, helped punch a hole through the air for Verstappen, giving him a tow through the final sector of the track, letting Verstappen claim second on the grid. On pole? Mercedes’ Kimi Antonelli yet again, with a margin of three tenths of a second to the Red Bull.

sentences.en: That qualifying session went rather well for the Dutch driver, too.
sentences.zh: 对于这位荷兰车手来说，那场排位赛也进展顺利。

sentences.en: His teammate, saddled with a grid penalty for changing an engine, helped punch a hole through the air for Verstappen, giving him a tow through the final sector of the track, letting Verstappen claim second on the grid.
sentences.zh: 他的队友因更换引擎而受到发车处罚，他帮助维斯塔潘在空中打了一个洞，将他拖过赛道的最后一段，让维斯塔潘获得了第二位。

sentences.en: On pole?
sentences.zh: 有吗？

sentences.en: Mercedes’ Kimi Antonelli yet again, with a margin of three tenths of a second to the Red Bull.
sentences.zh: 梅赛德斯车队的基米·安东内利再次领先红牛车队十分之三秒。

blocks.html_en: F1 is another thing AI has ruined

sentences.en: F1 is another thing AI has ruined
sentences.zh: F1是人工智能毁掉的又一件事

blocks.html_en: Things did not go nearly as well for Antonelli’s teammate, George Russell. Declared this year’s champion-in-waiting before the start of the season, the British driver has been comprehensively outdriven by his teenage teammate this year. But Antonelli’s ease with the new cars and Russell’s discomfort with having to adopt a radically new driving style can’t explain the half-second gap between the pair in qualifying.

sentences.en: Things did not go nearly as well for Antonelli’s teammate, George Russell.
sentences.zh: 对于安东内利的队友乔治·拉塞尔来说，事情并没有那么顺利。

sentences.en: Declared this year’s champion-in-waiting before the start of the season, the British driver has been comprehensively outdriven by his teenage teammate this year.
sentences.zh: 这位英国车手在赛季开始前就被宣布为今年的候补冠军，但今年他的表现却被他十几岁的队友全面超越。

sentences.en: But Antonelli’s ease with the new cars and Russell’s discomfort with having to adopt a radically new driving style can’t explain the half-second gap between the pair in qualifying.
sentences.zh: 但安东内利对新车的轻松驾驭以及拉塞尔对必须采用全新驾驶方式的不适并不能解释两人在排位赛中半秒的差距。

blocks.html_en: Over the weekend, the blame seemed to shift from Russell’s car to the driver, then back to the car again. There was—and is—something definitely wrong with Russell’s car, which was clearly slower than its sister car on several flat-out sections.

sentences.en: Over the weekend, the blame seemed to shift from Russell’s car to the driver, then back to the car again.
sentences.zh: 周末，责任似乎从拉塞尔的车转移到了司机身上，然后又回到了车上。

sentences.en: There was—and is—something definitely wrong with Russell’s car, which was clearly slower than its sister car on several flat-out sections.
sentences.zh: 拉塞尔的车过去和现在肯定有问题，在几个全速行驶的路段上，它明显比它的姊妹车慢。

blocks.html_en: The self-learning AI systems that teams are now using to manage their energy deployment strategies across a lap are looking increasingly likely to blame. By their nature, they’re fairly opaque to the driver. And because the electrical energy is so limited—just 4 MJ capacity in the battery—even tiny deployments to fill in extra torque to help the V6 or from getting a bit of wheelspin is enough to throw off the energy management and leave a driver with a fraction of the power they thought they should have.

sentences.en: The self-learning AI systems that teams are now using to manage their energy deployment strategies across a lap are looking increasingly likely to blame.
sentences.zh: 车队现在用来管理一圈能量部署策略的自学习人工智能系统似乎越来越有可能受到指责。

sentences.en: By their nature, they’re fairly opaque to the driver.
sentences.zh: 就其本质而言，它们对驾驶员来说相当不透明。

sentences.en: And because the electrical energy is so limited—just 4 MJ capacity in the battery—even tiny deployments to fill in extra torque to help the V6 or from getting a bit of wheelspin is enough to throw off the energy management and leave a driver with a fraction of the power they thought they should have.
sentences.zh: 而且由于电能非常有限——电池中的容量只有 4 兆焦耳——即使是微小的部署来填充额外的扭矩以帮助 V6 发动机或获得一点车轮打滑，也足以摆脱能源管理，让驾驶员只得到他们认为应该拥有的一小部分动力。

blocks.html_en: “My whole focus for the last 36 hours has been on straight-line speed. It hasn’t been focused on the set-up, tires, or anything, because we’re all trying to solve what is going on,” Russell told the media after qualifying.

sentences.en: “My whole focus for the last 36 hours has been on straight-line speed.
sentences.zh: “过去 36 小时我的全部注意力都集中在直线速度上。

sentences.en: It hasn’t been focused on the set-up, tires, or anything, because we’re all trying to solve what is going on,” Russell told the media after qualifying.
sentences.zh: 我们并没有把重点放在设置、轮胎或其他任何事情上，因为我们都在努力解决正在发生的事情，”拉塞尔在排位赛后告诉媒体。

blocks.html_en: “Even my last lap, for some reason, I lost another tenth and a half to myself, just on the straight,” he said. “And you’re watching on your steering wheel, [you’re] losing speed when you’re full gas on the straight. You feel powerless. We don’t know what’s going on. I don’t think it’s the power unit, to be honest. My team are really, really on it now, to try and solve it.”

sentences.en: “Even my last lap, for some reason, I lost another tenth and a half to myself, just on the straight,” he said.
sentences.zh: “即使是我的最后一圈，出于某种原因，我又在直道上失去了十分之一半，”他说。

sentences.en: “And you’re watching on your steering wheel, [you’re] losing speed when you’re full gas on the straight.
sentences.zh: “当你在直道上加满油时，你会看着方向盘，[你]会失去速度。

sentences.en: You feel powerless.
sentences.zh: 你感到无能为力。

sentences.en: We don’t know what’s going on.
sentences.zh: 我们不知道发生了什么事。

sentences.en: I don’t think it’s the power unit, to be honest.
sentences.zh: 老实说，我不认为这是动力装置。

sentences.en: My team are really, really on it now, to try and solve it.”
sentences.zh: 我的团队现在真的非常非常努力地尝试解决这个问题。”

blocks.html_en: His team did not solve it, and his car was visibly slower than the others as they flew down the Kemmel Straight toward the right-left of Les Combes. (Incidentally, isn’t it wonderful how all of Spa’s corners have actual names and aren’t just referred to as turn 1, turn 2, etc.?) As all the drivers lifted and coasted into what was previously a heavy braking zone, Russell regenerated enough energy to attempt an overtake on Lewis Hamilton’s Ferrari. Hamilton, minding his own business, had nowhere to go, and Russell clipped him and then spun into the gravel trap. An expletive-filled radio tirade back to the Mercedes garage followed, with Russell furious that yet again he’s rewarded with a DNF due to his car.

sentences.en: His team did not solve it, and his car was visibly slower than the others as they flew down the Kemmel Straight toward the right-left of Les Combes.
sentences.zh: 他的团队没有解决这个问题，当他们沿着凯梅尔直道向莱孔布的左右两侧飞去时，他的车明显比其他人慢。

sentences.en: (Incidentally, isn’t it wonderful how all of Spa’s corners have actual names and aren’t just referred to as turn 1, turn 2, etc.?)
sentences.zh: （顺便说一句，斯帕的所有弯道都有真实的名称，而不仅仅是称为“1 号弯”、“2 号弯”等，这不是很奇妙吗？）

sentences.en: As all the drivers lifted and coasted into what was previously a heavy braking zone, Russell regenerated enough energy to attempt an overtake on Lewis Hamilton’s Ferrari.
sentences.zh: 当所有车手抬起并滑行进入之前的重制动区域时，拉塞尔恢复了足够的能量，试图超越刘易斯·汉密尔顿的法拉利。

sentences.en: Hamilton, minding his own business, had nowhere to go, and Russell clipped him and then spun into the gravel trap.
sentences.zh: 汉密尔顿只顾自己的事，无处可去，拉塞尔撞倒了他，然后旋转进了砾石陷阱。

sentences.en: An expletive-filled radio tirade back to the Mercedes garage followed, with Russell furious that yet again he’s rewarded with a DNF due to his car.
sentences.zh: 随后，一段充满咒骂的无线电长篇大论回到梅赛德斯车库，拉塞尔对他再次因为他的车而获得 DNF 感到愤怒。

blocks.html_en: Lewis Hamilton would have been justified in firing off a few expletives himself. Despite the turn 1 collision being clearly a racing incident, the stewards decided it was somehow Hamilton’s fault and issued the driver a 5-second penalty.

sentences.en: Lewis Hamilton would have been justified in firing off a few expletives himself.
sentences.zh: 刘易斯·汉密尔顿本人也有理由咒骂几句。

sentences.en: Despite the turn 1 collision being clearly a racing incident, the stewards decided it was somehow Hamilton’s fault and issued the driver a 5-second penalty.
sentences.zh: 尽管一号弯的碰撞显然是一场赛车事故，赛会干事认为这在某种程度上是汉密尔顿的过错，并对车手进行了 5 秒的处罚。

blocks.html_en: Another win for Kimi Antonelli.

sentences.en: Another win for Kimi Antonelli.
sentences.zh: 基米·安东内利的又一场胜利。

blocks.html_en: Credit: Nicolas Economou/NurPhoto via Getty Images

sentences.en: Credit: Nicolas Economou/NurPhoto via Getty Images
sentences.zh: 图片来源：Nicolas Economou/NurPhoto 来自 Getty Images

sentences.en: Another win for Kimi Antonelli.
sentences.zh: 基米·安东内利的又一场胜利。

sentences.en: Credit: Nicolas Economou/NurPhoto via Getty Images
sentences.zh: 图片来源：Nicolas Economou/NurPhoto 来自 Getty Images

blocks.html_en: Another win for Kimi Antonelli. Credit: Nicolas Economou/NurPhoto via Getty Images

sentences.en: Another win for Kimi Antonelli.
sentences.zh: 基米·安东内利的又一场胜利。

sentences.en: Credit: Nicolas Economou/NurPhoto via Getty Images
sentences.zh: 图片来源：Nicolas Economou/NurPhoto 来自 Getty Images

blocks.html_en: The race was won, as so many others this season, by Antonelli. Verstappen thrilled his many fans in the crowd by briefly passing Antonelli at the start, a position he couldn’t hold. Antonelli was in the lead by Les Combes, and that’s where he finished the race, notching up his sixth win of the season. Although there were a couple of memorable overtakes, the 2026 race was far from a classic; it was one more sad example of how the new ruleset’s cars just don’t work properly on sport’s most popular race tracks. I guess I picked the wrong year to plan a trip to Monza for the Italian Grand Prix in September.

sentences.en: The race was won, as so many others this season, by Antonelli.
sentences.zh: 与本赛季的许多其他比赛一样，安东内利赢得了这场比赛。

sentences.en: Verstappen thrilled his many fans in the crowd by briefly passing Antonelli at the start, a position he couldn’t hold.
sentences.zh: 维斯塔潘在比赛开始时短暂地超越了安东内利，令观众中的众多粉丝兴奋不已，但他无法保持这个位置。

sentences.en: Antonelli was in the lead by Les Combes, and that’s where he finished the race, notching up his sixth win of the season.
sentences.zh: 安东内利在莱斯库姆 (Les Combes) 的领先下完成了比赛，取得了本赛季的第六场胜利。

sentences.en: Although there were a couple of memorable overtakes, the 2026 race was far from a classic; it was one more sad example of how the new ruleset’s cars just don’t work properly on sport’s most popular race tracks.
sentences.zh: 尽管有几次令人难忘的超车，但 2026 年的比赛远非经典。这是一个更悲惨的例子，说明新规则集的赛车在体育界最受欢迎的赛道上无法正常工作。

sentences.en: I guess I picked the wrong year to plan a trip to Monza for the Italian Grand Prix in September.
sentences.zh: 我想我计划去蒙扎参加九月份的意大利大奖赛的年份选错了。

blocks.html_en: Jonathan is the Automotive Editor at Ars Technica. He has a BSc and PhD in Pharmacology. In 2014 he decided to indulge his lifelong passion for the car by leaving the National Human Genome Research Institute and launching Ars Technica's automotive coverage. He lives in Washington, DC.

sentences.en: Jonathan is the Automotive Editor at Ars Technica.
sentences.zh: 乔纳森 (Jonathan) 是 Ars Technica 的汽车编辑。

sentences.en: He has a BSc and PhD in Pharmacology.
sentences.zh: 他拥有药理学学士和博士学位。

sentences.en: In 2014 he decided to indulge his lifelong passion for the car by leaving the National Human Genome Research Institute and launching Ars Technica's automotive coverage.
sentences.zh: 2014 年，他决定离开国家人类基因组研究所并推出 Ars Technica 的汽车报道，以沉溺于对汽车毕生的热情。

sentences.en: He lives in Washington, DC.
sentences.zh: 他住在华盛顿特区。

blocks.html_en: 115 Comments

sentences.en: 115 Comments
sentences.zh: 115 条评论

content_html_zh:
我试着保持积极的态度

许多人会说斯帕是地球上最好的赛道，但对于这些 F1 赛车来说却并非如此。

基米·安东内利 (Kimi Antonelli) 接近著名的 Eau Rouge 弯道。 图片来源：Nicolas Economou/NurPhoto 来自 Getty Images

基米·安东内利 (Kimi Antonelli) 接近著名的 Eau Rouge 弯道。 图片来源：Nicolas Economou/NurPhoto 来自 Getty Images

一级方程式赛车本周末重返斯帕-弗朗科尔尚赛道，参加比利时大奖赛。 这条位于阿登地区的停机坪带是这项运动历史悠久的场地之一，早在 1950 年的第一个 F1 赛季就曾在此举办过。 但正如昨天的比赛所表明的那样，当前的 F1 机械完全不适合这个戏剧性的场地。

正如我们今年早些时候在日本铃鹿车展上看到的那样，2026 F1 电池每圈只能为赛车的电动机提供几秒钟的动力；在剩下的一圈中，这些能源匮乏的赛车后轮的动力比初级 F2 支线系列赛要少。

世界上最好的赛道

对于许多人来说，包括目前的 F1 赛道中的大部分人，斯帕赛道可能被认为是地球上最伟大的赛道。 事实上，斯帕赛道和比利时大奖赛的举办时间都早于 F1 世界锦标赛，首场比赛于 1922 年举行。 当时，这条赛道是一条 9 英里长的公路环路，经过马尔梅迪和斯塔沃洛镇，然后返回弗朗科尔尚。 战后几年，布局略微缩小至 8.7 英里（14.1 公里），并且速度极快，平均单圈速度高达 150 英里/小时（240 公里/小时），而且由于汽车拥有巨大的动力和对安全性的最小让步，因此非常危险。

我们今天所熟知和喜爱的斯帕赛道于 1979 年进行了重新配置，削减了大约一半的原始布局，尽管它的长度为 4.3 英里（6.9 公里），但它仍然是 F1 日历上最长的赛道。 有趣的是，直到 2000 年，部分赛道仍然是公共道路，之后才成为专用赛道。 Eau Rouge 和 Raidillon 是其最著名的两个弯道 - 快速向左，然后向右，爬上 77 英尺（24 m）的陡峭山坡。 但在高下压力赛车时代，像普洪这样的其他弯道已经成为对技术的更大考验。

今年不行。 正如我已经多次详述的那样，目前的 F1 赛车根本不适合具有高速弯道的赛道。 由于新动力装置的混合动力侧提供的能量很少，因此一直全速行驶并不是完成一圈的最快方法。 曾经需要驾驶员投入精力、甚至需要一点勇气才能在极限状态下行驶的弯道，现在可以通过滑行进入，让发动机转移部分动力为电池充电。 显然，司机们并不兴奋。

红牛车队的马克斯·维斯塔潘 (Max Verstappen) 表示：“在第二部分的大部分时间里，你只需要依靠引擎来行驶。” “那那是什么？ 450马力，500马力？ 类似的东西，我猜，或多或少是三级方程式赛车所拥有的，但具有 F1 下压力。 当然，你可以想象，驾驶起来并不是很令人兴奋。”

“但说实话，我不想再坐在这里抱怨，因为可能有人会在门外向我开枪，”他开玩笑说。

关于维斯塔潘未来的谣言仍在不断流传。 由于他不在车手积分榜前两名，他可能会激活红牛合同中的豁免条款，一些人认为转会迈凯轮是可能的。 完全的 F1 休假是另一种选择，因为维斯塔潘毫不掩饰他想要赢得 F1 之外世界上其他一些伟大比赛的渴望，比如勒芒 24 小时耐力赛、纽博格林赛道，当然还有斯帕赛道。

虽然我很想看到维斯塔潘参加更多 GT3 比赛，甚至可能参加原型车比赛，但我猜他会留在 F1 和红牛车队。 “我只是在精神上适应它。 我正在努力充分利用它，”他在排位赛后告诉媒体。 “当然，尽管这不是我喜欢的，也不是我在一级方程式赛车中喜欢做的。 但我也可以坐在家里什么都不开车。 但这也没有任何作用。 所以我只是尽力而为。”

对于这位荷兰车手来说，那场排位赛也进展顺利。 他的队友因更换引擎而受到发车处罚，他帮助维斯塔潘在空中打了一个洞，将他拖过赛道的最后一段，让维斯塔潘获得了第二位。 有吗？ 梅赛德斯车队的基米·安东内利再次领先红牛车队十分之三秒。

F1是人工智能毁掉的又一件事

对于安东内利的队友乔治·拉塞尔来说，事情并没有那么顺利。 这位英国车手在赛季开始前就被宣布为今年的候补冠军，但今年他的表现却被他十几岁的队友全面超越。 但安东内利对新车的轻松驾驭以及拉塞尔对必须采用全新驾驶方式的不适并不能解释两人在排位赛中半秒的差距。

周末，责任似乎从拉塞尔的车转移到了司机身上，然后又回到了车上。 拉塞尔的车过去和现在肯定有问题，在几个全速行驶的路段上，它明显比它的姊妹车慢。

车队现在用来管理一圈能量部署策略的自学习人工智能系统似乎越来越有可能受到指责。 就其本质而言，它们对驾驶员来说相当不透明。 而且由于电能非常有限——电池中的容量只有 4 兆焦耳——即使是微小的部署来填充额外的扭矩以帮助 V6 发动机或获得一点车轮打滑，也足以摆脱能源管理，让驾驶员只得到他们认为应该拥有的一小部分动力。

“过去 36 小时我的全部注意力都集中在直线速度上。 我们并没有把重点放在设置、轮胎或其他任何事情上，因为我们都在努力解决正在发生的事情，”拉塞尔在排位赛后告诉媒体。

“即使是我的最后一圈，出于某种原因，我又在直道上失去了十分之一半，”他说。 “当你在直道上加满油时，你会看着方向盘，[你]会失去速度。 你感到无能为力。 我们不知道发生了什么事。 老实说，我不认为这是动力装置。 我的团队现在真的非常非常努力地尝试解决这个问题。”

他的团队没有解决这个问题，当他们沿着凯梅尔直道向莱孔布的左右两侧飞去时，他的车明显比其他人慢。 （顺便说一句，斯帕的所有弯道都有真实的名称，而不仅仅是称为“1 号弯”、“2 号弯”等，这不是很奇妙吗？） 当所有车手抬起并滑行进入之前的重制动区域时，拉塞尔恢复了足够的能量，试图超越刘易斯·汉密尔顿的法拉利。 汉密尔顿只顾自己的事，无处可去，拉塞尔撞倒了他，然后旋转进了砾石陷阱。 随后，一段充满咒骂的无线电长篇大论回到梅赛德斯车库，拉塞尔对他再次因为他的车而获得 DNF 感到愤怒。

刘易斯·汉密尔顿本人也有理由咒骂几句。 尽管一号弯的碰撞显然是一场赛车事故，赛会干事认为这在某种程度上是汉密尔顿的过错，并对车手进行了 5 秒的处罚。

与本赛季的许多其他比赛一样，安东内利赢得了这场比赛。 维斯塔潘在比赛开始时短暂地超越了安东内利，令观众中的众多粉丝兴奋不已，但他无法保持这个位置。 安东内利在莱斯库姆 (Les Combes) 的领先下完成了比赛，取得了本赛季的第六场胜利。 尽管有几次令人难忘的超车，但 2026 年的比赛远非经典。这是一个更悲惨的例子，说明新规则集的赛车在体育界最受欢迎的赛道上无法正常工作。 我想我计划去蒙扎参加九月份的意大利大奖赛的年份选错了。

乔纳森 (Jonathan) 是 Ars Technica 的汽车编辑。 他拥有药理学学士和博士学位。 2014 年，他决定离开国家人类基因组研究所并推出 Ars Technica 的汽车报道，以沉溺于对汽车毕生的热情。 他住在华盛顿特区。
